introduces: ALTER TABLE public.pick_candidates ADD COLUMN sport_key text NULL
surface_mappings.exact_recommended_next_action: Lowest-risk direct candidate. Operator may approve applying the migration file after a final dry-run/review because it only adds a nullable column and index on an existing table. Re-run a live schema probe immediately after apply.

Do not apply the file blindly. First verify the live state of the local-only-but-semantic-live UTV2-772 prerequisites, then prepare an operator-reviewed object-level delta for the missing table/function and separately decide whether the cron job body should be changed in the same window.

soft_dependencies: provider_offer_line_snapshots table from the same migration
surface_mappings.exact_recommended_next_action: Treat as part of the provider-retention manual delta, not as a stand-alone direct apply. The function performs INSERT/UPSERT writes into provider_offer_line_snapshots and should only be introduced after the target table and partitioned history baseline are re-verified.

Do not approve direct file apply from this plan alone. Although the migration itself only defines a function, that function drops partitions when called. Reconcile it as part of the provider-retention slice only after the operator accepts the partitioning baseline and summarization path.

introduces: ADD CONSTRAINT picks_stake_units_canonical_check CHECK (stake_units is not null and stake_units > 0) NOT VALID
surface_mappings.exact_recommended_next_action: Good direct candidate after sport_key. The constraint is NOT VALID and intentionally avoids rewriting historical rows, so the operator can review and approve a normal migration apply with low blast radius.

introduces: ALTER TABLE public.pick_candidates ADD COLUMN model_registry_id UUID REFERENCES public.model_registry(id)
surface_mappings.exact_recommended_next_action: Do not approve yet. Ownership should remain blocked until the earlier missing low-risk schema surfaces are reconciled and the operator explicitly resumes the UTV2-855 ownership rollout.

introduces: ALTER TABLE public.pick_candidates ADD COLUMN scoring_run_id UUID REFERENCES public.system_runs(id)
surface_mappings.exact_recommended_next_action: Same as the ownership slice overall: defer until the operator approves the resumed UTV2-855 ownership rollout after earlier schema convergence.

introduces: ALTER TABLE public.pick_candidates ADD COLUMN ownership_timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ
surface_mappings.exact_recommended_next_action: Same as the ownership slice overall: defer until the operator approves the resumed UTV2-855 ownership rollout after earlier schema convergence.

introduces: ALTER TABLE public.model_registry ADD COLUMN registry_entity_type TEXT
surface_mappings.exact_recommended_next_action: Defer with the ownership slice. This column is part of the same migration that also normalizes existing model_registry rows.

introduces: ALTER TABLE public.model_registry ADD COLUMN source_type_compatibility TEXT[]
surface_mappings.exact_recommended_next_action: Defer with the ownership slice. The live rollout should handle the full model_registry metadata normalization in one approved window.

introduces: ALTER TABLE public.model_registry ADD COLUMN active_state TEXT
surface_mappings.exact_recommended_next_action: Defer with the ownership slice. This column changes runtime ownership gating and should not move forward until the operator explicitly resumes UTV2-855.

related_local_migration_not_required_for.why_not_in_immediate_apply_set: It mutates historical pick_candidates rows and is not required to create any of the listed missing live surfaces.
related_local_migration_not_required_for.recommended_next_action: Defer. Reassess only after the schema-only and constraint-only surfaces are reconciled and the operator explicitly wants the historical linkage backfill.

indirect_or_semantic_dependencies.why_it_matters: The drop function assumes the partitioned provider_offer_history baseline exists. Phase 6 confirmed that provider_offer_history exists live even though 202604291002 is missing from the remote ledger, so operator reconciliation must trust object-level live truth before touching the retention slice.

The cron body calls prune_provider_offers_bounded(), and summarize_provider_offer_history_partition() reads provider_offer_history partitions. Those prerequisites are not remote-ledger aligned even though key live objects already exist.

soft_runtime_dependency_findings.finding: UTV2-846 added a schema-cache fallback because runtime observed sport_key failure on the DB path. Phase 6 later confirmed the physical column is absent live, so reconciliation should treat this as a real schema gap, not a cache-only issue.
UTV2-845 runtime proof passed without the DB constraint being present live. The missing constraint is therefore a convergence gap, not an acute runtime blocker.
Ownership proof shows 0% ownership write success while columns are absent; rollout must stay blocked until earlier convergence is complete.

recommended_apply_order.why_first: Smallest runtime-critical convergence gap. It aligns live schema with current DB types and removes the root physical gap behind the UTV2-846 fallback.

recommended_apply_order.why_second: The constraint is intentionally NOT VALID and does not rewrite historical rows. It is a low-blast-radius convergence step after sport_key.

recommended_apply_order.why_third: Before touching 202605030001/202605030002, the operator should explicitly accept the live/object truth of 202604291001/202604291002/202604291003 because that slice is already partially present live but absent from the remote ledger.

recommended_apply_order.why_fourth: This slice mixes tables, functions, cron mutation, and destructive future behavior. It should be reconciled as a tightly reviewed operator window, not a blind normal push.

recommended_apply_order.why_fifth: Ownership remains intentionally blocked until earlier missing surfaces are reconciled and the operator explicitly restarts the UTV2-855 rollout.

recommended_apply_order.why_last_or_defer: Not required for the listed missing surfaces and mutates historical rows. Keep it outside the immediate schema-reconciliation window unless separately approved.

operator_decision_plan.overall_risk_classification: D3 remains in force until the earlier missing low-risk surfaces are reconciled and the provider-retention slice is explicitly reviewed.
operator_decision_plan.any_live_write_can_be_approved_now: Not from this planning artifact alone. The plan identifies two lower-risk direct candidates, but actual approval remains an operator decision after reviewing dry-run/output and the provider-retention drift context.
